summary: This cluster focuses on the restoration and establishment of the brown alga *Laminaria japonica* in coastal environments, with a shared emphasis on the physical and mechanical factors influencing transplant success. Key findings demonstrate that longer initial blade lengths (30 cm) significantly improve survival rates and drag force compared to shorter transplants, and that optimizing plate weight (508.2 g per 18.11 cm blade width) enhances stability on concrete substrates. The research is geographically and ecologically centered on temperate coastal waters, addressing practical methods for resource enhancement through controlled aquarium and field-based transplanting experiments.
